texto: I learned that the day "The Viper's Drag" slipped from between my fingers. But whatever might be lost or broken or forgotten is nothing compared to the miraculous rebirth that occurs every time the needle hits the groove. Here is Fats Waller Himself, not dead but present, so present that he overwhelms the well-ordered precincts of the living room. The sound sprawls. What vibrates here has more life than any room.
explicacao: Citação de Geoffrey O'Brien sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “I learned that the day "The Viper's Drag" slipped from between my fingers. But whatever might be …” articula vida, magic, musical-favorites em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como musical-favorites e rebirth.

frase_original: I learned that the day "The Viper's Drag" slipped from between my fingers. But whatever might be lost or broken or forgotten is nothing compared to the miraculous rebirth that occurs every time the needle hits the groove. Here is Fats Waller Himself, not dead but present, so present that he overwhelms the well-ordered precincts of the living room. The sound sprawls. What vibrates here has more life than any room.

texto: To attempt to describe how music pervades and flavors a life feels a little like an invasion of privacy, even if the privacy is my own. Listening to music,...is finally the most inward of acts--so inward that even language, even the language of thought, can come to seem intrusive...After all these procedures the unbreachable mysteriousness of music remains intact. The book can never be more than an interruption. Afterward, the listening begins again, to generate, in turn, other and completely different books.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Geoffrey O'Brien: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “To attempt to describe how music pervades and flavors a life feels a little like an invasion of p…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, imagination, listening pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de listening e music.

texto: The age of recording is necessarily an age of nostalgia--when was the past so hauntingly accessible?--but its bitterest insight is the incapacity of even the most perfectly captured sound to restore the moment of its first inscribing. That world is no longer there.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Geoffrey O'Brien que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “The age of recording is necessarily an age of nostalgia--when was the past so hauntingly accessib…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre tempo, captured, nostalgia, com foco em nostalgia e recording-music.

texto: The question of what exactly we remember when we listen to old recordings, or whether it can be called remembering at all, becomes less and less answerable over a lifetime.
explicacao: Citação de Geoffrey O'Brien sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “The question of what exactly we remember when we listen to old recordings, or whether it can be c…” articula reflexao, memory, remembering em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como remembering e unknown.

texto: Scattered among these things are reminders that sound once existed: a metronome, a drumming pad, a guitar pick, a trumpet mouthpiece, a music stand, a tuning fork, a block of rosin...The older instruments bear the marks of those who have already played them, the scuffs and bites and dents that are the mysterious scars of sound. In their midst the house hangs, tenuous and enveloping, a sounding board waiting to be struck.
explicacao: Citação de Geoffrey O'Brien sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Scattered among these things are reminders that sound once existed: a metronome, a drumming pad, …” articula reflexao, artifacts, instruments em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como instruments e mark.
